os: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.4.z for Power, Little Endian
symptoms: watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#19 stuck for 23s! [migration/19:127]
rcu: INFO: rcu_sched detected stalls on CPUs/tasks:
CPU 8 didn't respond to backtrace IPI, inspecting paca.

workflow.thought: Search kernel log for soft lockup and rcu_sched stall messages
workflow.action: log | grep -e rcu_sched\ detected -e soft\ lockup
workflow.observation: Multiple soft lockup messages and rcu_sched CPU stall messages

workflow.thought: Examine multi_stop_data structure to understand CPU stop operation
workflow.observation: active_cpus field pointing to cpumask structure

workflow.thought: Check cpumask to see which CPUs are active
workflow.observation: bits field showing CPU 8 is set

workflow.thought: Evaluate binary value to confirm CPU 8 is the active CPU
workflow.observation: Binary shows bit 8 is set (CPU 8)

workflow.thought: Check per-CPU call_single_queue for pending IPI requests
workflow.observation: Only CPU 8 has pending items in call_single_queue

workflow.thought: Examine pending call_single_data_t structures for CPU 8
workflow.observation: Multiple do_exit_flush_lazy_tlb and cpu_get_purr functions pending

workflow.thought: Check active CPUs in another multi_stop_data instance
workflow.observation: active_cpus field pointing to different cpumask

workflow.thought: Examine second cpumask structure
workflow.observation: bits field showing CPU 48 is set

workflow.thought: Evaluate binary value to confirm CPU 48 is set
workflow.observation: Binary shows bit 48 is set (CPU 48)

workflow.thought: Get backtrace of CPU 8 to see what it's doing
workflow.observation: CPU 8 stuck in plpar_hcall_norets+28, __spin_yield+148

workflow.thought: Check process state for task on CPU 8
workflow.observation: Task is in WAIT state on CPU 8

workflow.thought: Disassemble update_blocked_averages to see spinlock call
workflow.observation: Call to _raw_spin_lock_irqsave at offset

workflow.thought: Find runqueue for CPU 63
workflow.observation: CPU 63 runqueue address found

workflow.thought: Get address of rq.lock spinlock for CPU 63
workflow.observation: Address of raw_spinlock_t structure

workflow.thought: Examine the actual spinlock value
workflow.observation: slock = 0x0 (spinlock is not locked)

workflow.thought: Disassemble __spin_yield to see hypervisor call
workflow.observation: Call to plpar_hcall_norets at offset

workflow.thought: Disassemble plpar_hcall_norets to see current execution point
workflow.observation: At lwz instruction after hypervisor call

workflow.thought: Check timer queue for CPU 8 to see overdue timers
workflow.observation: Multiple overdue timers in hrtimer queue

workflow.thought: Check sysctl setting for rcu stall panic
workflow.observation: sysctl_panic_on_rcu_stall = 0

workflow.thought: Check sysctl setting for softlockup panic
workflow.observation: softlockup_panic = 0

root_cause_analysis: The CPU is not processing timer interrupts properly due to some hardware glitch.
CPU 8 is somehow going wrong with taking the rq.lock spinlock of CPU 63. It's getting stuck waiting on the spinlock that is not being locked.
Due to this problematic condition, it's not able to process timer interrupts properly and thus many overdue timers are in hrtimer queue.
The issue is of faulty hardware (CPU 8).
